embeds.description:
Master Ruhk serves as one of five Jedi on the Masters Council, the informal decision-making body of Force-users, scholars, and shipboard personnel tasked with the daily operations and mission direction of the *Chu'unthor II*. An accomplished battlefield commander, Master Ruhk left the mainstream Jedi Order to join the Altisian Sect at the height of the Clone War. Rumors abound as to his reasoning, although the most prevailing whisper speaks of the mighty Feeorin's public denouncing of the Republic after a plague of bureaucratic incompetence caused the death of nearly his entire clone trooper battalion. 
Stalwart, brusque, and blunt as a rockface, Master Ruhk serves as a prudent anchor against the loftier ideals and philosophies of the other masters on board. In his role as the Battlemaster for the Jedi onboard the *Chu'unthor*, Ruhk is a firm believer that physical toil paves the way to spiritual enlightenment. His training is strict and arduous, but ultimately fair and worthwhile, and no one more than the Feeorin Master commends hard work and diligence. He has acquired a reputation for shaping exemplary Jedi from the most difficult of Padawans and Initiates, and it is said that Master Ruhk has a soft-spot for orphans, strays, and misfits.

Adalyn is a 15-year-old Jedi Padawan, marked by privilege and loss.  Born into the esteemed House Pallax of the Tapani Sector, Adalyn's early life was one of privilege and rigorous education.  However, her world was shattered by the Clone Wars, during which she lost her first Jedi Master and her right arm.  She is now training under Master Ruhk, a Jedi Masterr known for his decisive action and combat prowess.  Adalyn is skilled in the Force, determined, and resilient, but also emotionally vulnerable.  She is on a journey of self-discovery, striving to become a Jedi Knight in the darkness of the galaxy.

Ash was born and raised on the rural world of Lucazec, a planet known for its lush forests and peaceful inhabitants. His parents were killed in a tragic accident when he was young, leaving him to fend for himself. He learned to survive on his own, developing a deep connection with the natural world and a strong sense of self-reliance. One day, Master Ruhk, a Jedi Master on a mission to Lucazec, discovered Ash and was impressed by his resilience and Force sensitivity. Master Ruhk took Ash under his wing, bringing him to the Jedi Temple to train as a Jedi Initiate.

Proclaiming themselves as the "Roughest and Toughest Clan You Ever Did See", the younglings of Zakkeg Clan are the *Chu'unthor's* most exuberant collection of initiates (overwhelmingly so, depending upon whom you speak with), under the tutelage of Master Ruhk. The smallest of all the Jedi initiate clans onboard the spacefaring academy, the younglings of Clan Zakkeg are a tight-knit group, and Rukh promotes their teamwork and camaraderie by encouraging them to work together in their training and the solving of their problems. Although the clan has a reputation for mischief, the Zakkeg's are truly good at heart, and Master Ruhk's stern teachings and steady guidance brings out the best in each initiate.

Independent and self-assured, the other youngling's seem to naturally gravitate around Jyzen, making him their leader. Before the beginning of the Clone Wars, an infant Jyzen was rescued from an Iridorian passenger shuttle that was left on failing life-support after a surprise Yinchorri pirate raid. While Jyzen may be the mastermind behind all of Clan Zakkeg's schemes, he is also the first to defend his friends, either through word or prank, and Master Ruhk holds the young Zabrak especially accountable when the Zakkeg's best-laid plans have gone awry.

During a mission to Nar Shaddaa, several years before the outbreak of the Clone War, an Altisian Master is "offered" a young Wexal by the Arconan's salt-addicted mother. Sensing the latent potential within the child, the Master takes Wexal as a mercy against the life he would succumb to on the Smuggler's Moon, andbegins to train him in the ways of the Force. 
If Jyzen Reus is Clan Zakkeg's leader, Wexal is the clan's ever resourceful "swingman", facilitating Jyzen's every scheme and always managing to get into places he shouldn't be. Although having grown-up mostly independent of an Arconan nest, when stressed or frightened Wexal unknowingly falls into the Arconan habit of referring to himself as "we", although he is so often caught red-handed along with the other younglings that the verbal miscue goes unnoticed.

Following a failed trade negotiation with the Czerka corporation on Belgaroth, Master Ruhk's luck worsens as his shuttle experiences a power coupling meltdown. The Feeorin Jedi is forced to seek replacement parts from the very many scrapyards surrounding the spaceport, but at every turn he finds nothing but greedy junk dealers, con artists, and roving "protection" gangs. Master Ruhk almost gives up hope, before happening upon a tiny junk lot in one of Belgaroth's forgotten neighborhoods. The junkyard is run by an elderly Rodian woman and her tiny granddaughter, who buzzes around the massive scrap heaps like a Ratitan whisperfly.
When Ruhk identifies the force potential in the youth, the grandmother gives Ruhk his parts for free and impresses upon the Jedi Master to take young Depik with him when he departs, as she hopes for a better life for Depik where her gifts won't go to squander. Energetic, attentive, and flighty, Depik shows an incredible affinity for all things mechanical and electronic, although this often times results in her taking apart or "improving" things that do not belong to her. She is clan Zakkeg's faithful gadgeteer, and no Zakkeg scheme is ever complete until the first "Depik-trademarked" contraption misfires.

Buthbo is the youngest and newest member of Clan Zakkeg. Master Ruhk finds her in the aftermath of a surprise Separatist raid on a neutral Ithorian medical colony, and the trauma of the violence and raw emotion Buthbo feels through her burgeoning senses in the Force leaves her with her throats partially-paralyzed, barely able to utter more than buzzing squeaks or groans of annoyance.
Shy and gentle of heart, Buthbo often times has to be "convinced" to go on Zakkeg adventures, even though she tends to have fun afterwards. Due to her slow feet and timid nature, Jyzen tends to use her as the lookout in their plots, although the clan's most infamous schemes are usually made so by flights of panicked courage by the Ithorian. As her power in the Force continues to grow, Buthbo is gaining a reputation for an affinity for life cultivation and healing, quickly becoming known as one of the best gardeners in the academy.

content:
## Chu'unthor ##
The Chu'unthor, the second ship to bear that name, was the wandering praxeum starship of the Jedi Djinn Altis during the Clone Wars.

embeds.description: A wise and contemplative Jedi Master, Valerius serves as the Sect's Loremaster. His deep understanding of Jedi philosophy, both orthodox and Altisian, makes him an invaluable guide for members seeking to reconcile their beliefs with Jedi principles. Valerius is known for his patient demeanor and insightful teachings, often drawing upon historical Jedi texts and personal experiences to illustrate his points. He emphasizes the importance of emotional intelligence and self-awareness in maintaining balance while fostering healthy connections. Within the Sect, Valerius oversees the Temple's archives and meditation practices, ensuring the Altisian Jedi remain grounded in their core values while exploring the nuances of the Force.

A skilled diplomat and warrior, Lissandra is the Sect's Master of Outreach. She embodies the Altisian ideal of engaging with the galaxy while maintaining Jedi discipline. Lissandra is responsible for the Sect's interactions with the wider galaxy, negotiating aid agreements, and representing the Altisian Jedi in intergalactic forums. Her approach is characterized by empathy and pragmatism, seeking to build bridges and foster understanding. Lissandra's combat skills are formidable, but she prioritizes de-escalation and peaceful resolutions, reflecting the Altisian commitment to protecting attachments and communities. She often leads missions to assist developing worlds, demonstrating the Sect's dedication to service and compassion.

Energetic and innovative, Rennak is the Sect's Master of Instruction. He is passionate about developing new training methods that align with Altisian values, focusing on emotional resilience and collaborative learning. Rennak believes in nurturing individual strengths while fostering a strong sense of community among Padawans. He is known for his unconventional teaching style, incorporating real-world experiences and scenario-based training to prepare young Jedi for the complexities of life with attachments. Rennak is also a skilled lightsaber duelist, favoring forms that emphasize fluidity and adaptability, mirroring the Altisian Sect's flexible approach to Jedi tradition. He represents the future of the Sect, embodying the potential for growth and evolution within the Altisian way.

Djinn Altis is the revered leader of the Altisian Jedi, a distinct sect operating outside the orthodox Jedi Order. Master Altis is known for his reformist views, challenging the traditional Jedi stance on attachment by openly encouraging relationships and families within his order. He believes in a more compassionate interpretation of the Jedi path and that individuals of any age can be trained in the Force, recruiting those old enough to choose their own destiny.  
The Altisian Jedi, under Altis's guidance, function somewhat independently, often serving as relief workers on planets in need rather than engaging directly in combat. Despite their divergence from the mainstream Jedi Order, they are not considered enemies and have even cooperated with the Jedi Council on occasion, particularly during times of crisis. However, their unorthodox practices and beliefs often lead to them being viewed as heretical or at least unconventional by more conservative Jedi.
Altis himself is described as a wise and influential figure, attracting a dedicated following to his teachings. He operates a mobile academy and maintains an enclave on the planet Bespin. While some may see his views as radical, he is generally respected for his commitment to helping others and his unique interpretation of the Jedi Code.

The human male commanding officer of the *Chu'unthor II*, Captain Aris Thorne is a veteran starship captain in his early 50s. Non-Force-sensitive, he possesses decades of experience and a calm demeanor under extreme pressure. Deeply loyal to Master Altis, he makes the hard calls regarding ship maneuvers and tactical decisions, his steady hand a source of confidence for the entire crew.

A human female in her mid-40s, Commander Heston serves as the Chief of Operations and Executive Officer aboard the *Chu'unthor II*. An ex-Republic Navy officer who joined the Altisians before Order 66, she is the logistical backbone of the fugitive fleet. Stern, impeccably organized, and pragmatic, she translates Council directives into actionable plans, managing everything from duty rosters to damage control with unwavering competence. While not known for warmth, her dedication to the ship and its diverse population is absolute, and she commands respect through her efficiency and fairness.

This human male Jedi Knight, in his late 30s, heads shipboard security. Vigilant, disciplined, and highly tactical, Knight Kress is responsible for internal patrols, managing the ship's defenses, and training a volunteer militia. His focus is grim given their situation, but he is known to be fair. He values combat skill and pragmatism, coordinating closely with other departments to ensure the *Chu'unthor's* safety.

A human male Jedi Knight in his late 20s, Jaro Keff is the Chief Tactical Officer on the *Chu'unthor II's* bridge. Sharp, quick-thinking, and with an instinctive grasp of ship-to-ship combat dynamics, he is calm and hyper-focused during crises. He advises Captain Thorne on tactical options, sometimes with a youthful eagerness for more aggressive solutions.

A young Mirialan female in her mid-20s, Elara Vayne is a non-Force-sensitive refugee who has quickly proven herself an indispensable tech whiz. She manages the *Chu'unthor II's* precarious communications, encryption, and basic signals intelligence. Sharp, quick-thinking, and dedicated, she works tirelessly to gather any scrap of reliable intel while maintaining vital comm silence, driven by a fierce desire to protect the fleet that gave her sanctuary.

An Arkanian Offshoot female Jedi Master of venerable age, Zylia Korta is the Chief Navigator. Her pale skin, white hair, and silver eyes lend her an ancient, almost otherworldly appearance. Her knowledge of esoteric astrogation and forgotten hyperspace routes is unparalleled and vital for guiding the fugitive fleet through uncharted regions like the Karthon Expanse. She is patient with dedicated learners but expects precision.

This Togruta female in her early 40s serves as the Refugee Coordinator and Civilian Affairs Liaison. A former planetary administrator, her natural Togruta empathy and skill as a mediator are invaluable in managing the needs and disputes of the diverse civilian population aboard the *Chu'unthor II*. She works tirelessly to foster community and advocate for the vulnerable.

A human female Jedi Knight in her early 30s, Jessa Wynn specializes in healing. Working under Chief Healer Vokara, she is a calm and compassionate presence in the medbay, tirelessly treating the wounded and managing the health of the ship's population with limited resources. Her gentle touch and ability to soothe pain both physically and through the Force make her a vital part of the community's resilience.

This Mon Calamari male, a veteran civilian engineer in his late 50s, is the gruff genius keeping the *Chu'unthor II* from falling apart. Overworked and perpetually muttering about failing systems and impossible demands, Rory Gaxx nevertheless performs daily miracles with scrap parts and sheer willpower. Fiercely protective of his engine room and his crew, he has little patience for anything but practical solutions but has served the Altisians loyally for years, possibly even aboard the original *Chu'unthor*.

A Duros male in his mid-50s, Boriv Tem is the Quartermaster, burdened with managing the *Chu'unthor II's* dwindling supplies. Meticulously organized and utterly pragmatic, he enforces rationing with a stern but fair hand, constantly battling the grim arithmetic of their survival. His decisions directly impact everyone aboard.

An elderly Bothan male, Cellan Ree is the Lead Archivist, assisting Master Vantos. Meticulous to the point of being pedantic, he is fiercely protective of the Jedi and Altisian knowledge under his care. He is a font of obscure historical information but can be wary of those who approach ancient records or artifacts without due reverence.

Master Chase Davion, a human Jedi Master in his early 50s, has recently taken on the mentorship of Master Rukh's students. He presents a calm, empathetic, and observant demeanor, often guiding through Socratic questioning rather than direct orders. His practical, worn Jedi robes and keen hazel eyes reflect a thoughtful and approachable nature. While not a member of the Altisian Council, he is a respected senior Master whose insights on training and student welfare are valued. He emphasizes mindfulness, emotional honesty, and adaptability, fostering a different style of resilience in his charges compared to more traditional, martial approaches.

A human male in his late 20s or early 30s, Kael Varro is an ex-Republic Navy scout pilot who recently found refuge with the Altisians. Wiry and sharp-eyed, he's a highly skilled pilot, pragmatic and cynical, with a "gut feeling" for danger in space. He's still grappling with the fall of the Republic but values competence and loyalty in those he works with.

This Zeltron female in her early 20s is a security specialist and expert slicer. Athletic and agile, she possesses the natural Zeltron charisma and empathy but also a steely core. She's skilled in close-quarters combat and tech infiltration, fiercely loyal to the Altisian community that gave her refuge, and passionate about protecting the vulnerable.

Intelligent, observant, and possessing a quiet resilience, Marina Cloudspinner's path to the Jedi was forged in early tragedy and loss. Her deep connection to the Force manifested not only in her Jedi training but also in rare psychometric abilities and an exceptionally strong, almost unheard-of, Force Bond with fellow Initiate Wes Del-Fin. A skilled mechanic and tactician, honed under her first Master, Firith Ven, until his death on Geonosis, Marina later served under Master Silas Draay on discreet missions. These experiences, coupled with closely guarded personal trials, have instilled in her a pragmatic approach to survival and a fierce, protective loyalty to those she considers family. Now with the Altisians, she seeks to balance her Jedi duties with the profound changes in her life, navigating the uncertain future with Wes by her side.

Stocky and weathered, Roric Jannsen is a man of few words and decisive action. His grey-streaked dark hair is practical, and his alert, no-nonsense eyes constantly scan for threats. Old scars, a thin one across his cheek and another on his knuckles, speak of a life lived on the rough edges of the galaxy. He carries himself with the quiet confidence of a veteran, his well-maintained blaster rifle a near-constant companion. Before finding his way to the Altisian fleet, Roric's experience was forged in the fires of planetary militias or corporate security details, making him a pragmatic and reliable guard in any tight spot.

Lean and agile, Tessa Raine moves with a quiet quickness that belies her sharp, perceptive gaze. Her dark hair is usually kept in a tight, practical braid, and her eyes miss little detail. Often preferring to observe from the periphery, "Echo" has a knack for navigating complex environments unseen and unheard, skills likely honed in the underlevels of a sprawling city-planet or during her time as a scout for a survey corps. She joined the Altisians seeking a more principled existence, offering her resourcefulness and talent for stealth to their cause.
